description: Guided scuba dive operations — reef dives, wreck dives, intro dives. Often includes equipment rental. Day-of manifest is safety-critical.
key_characteristics: Equipment rental and allocation per booking (tanks, wetsuits, fins)
Mixed ability groups — certified divers vs intro/discover divers
Medical waivers and health declarations needed
Day-of manifest is safety-critical — guides need it before they leave the dock
pain_points: Day-of manifest can't be customised per activity type in most booking software — dive operators need fields for cert level, equipment size, experience
Equipment allocation tracked manually or in a spreadsheet outside the booking system
Certification verification collected on the day, not at booking — creates day-of admin
Mixed groups require manual splitting or separate products in the booking system
International pre-arrival bookings are hard without a clean direct booking flow — dive tourists book months ahead
general_discovery_questions: How do you manage equipment allocation for your dives — is that in your booking system or something separate?
When you have a group with mixed cert levels, how does your manifest handle that?
Do you collect diver certifications at the point of booking or on the day?
How do you handle last-minute cancellations when equipment has already been pulled for the day?
fareharbor_strengths: Fully customisable manifest fields per activity — cert level, equipment size, experience notes
Waiver and health declaration collection at booking
